content:
Let’s place the rectangle so that  
A=(0,0), B=(5,0), C=(5,12), D=(0,12).  
We are given a point E whose distance to AB (the x–axis) is 12 and to AD (the y–axis) is 1, lying “below” the rectangle so that ∆ABE is acute.  One checks that the only choice is  
E=(1,−12).  

We seek the line through E that meets AB at F=(f,0) and CD at G=(g,12) and cuts the 5×12 rectangle into two regions of area 30 each.  Label R₁ the quadrilateral F–B–C–G; its area is by the shoelace formula  
Area(R₁)=½|(f·0+5·12+5·12+g·0)−(0·5+0·5+12·g+12·f)|  
         =½|120 −12(f+g)|.  
Setting this equal to 30 gives 120−12(f+g)=60 ⇒ f+g=5.  

Collinearity of F, E, G gives the slope condition  
(−12−0)/(1−f) = (12−(−12))/(g−1)  
⇒ −12/(1−f) = 24/(g−1)  
⇒ 2(1−f)=g−1  ⇒ g=2f−1.  

Together with f+g=5 ⇒ f+(2f−1)=5 ⇒3f=6⇒f=2, hence g=3.  
Thus  
F=(2,0),  G=(3,12),  
so  
FG²=(3−2)²+(12−0)²=1+144=145.  

Answer:  \boxed{145}.
